bindings.quest_action: perform a cleansing ritual at the old temple shrine
bindings.quest_focus: a disturbed shrine deep in the temple that unleashed a spreading curse
bindings.quest_stakes: the land itself is dying: crops blacken, wells taste of copper
bindings.quest_threat: an ancient force angered by the desecration of its sacred ground
bindings.quest_origin: something disturbed the shrine and now the corruption spreads outward

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, have you noticed the sky over {location}? That sickly green light isn't natural. Nothing about what's happening here is natural.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The land itself is turning against us. {quest_stakes}. {target_npc} says the spirits that guard this region have been angered: {quest_origin}. We need {quest_item} to {quest_action} before the corruption reaches the settlement. The {enemy_type} spawning near the temple are a symptom, not the cause.
dialogueChunks.outro: I don't pretend to understand the divine. But {quest_threat} is real, and {quest_focus} must be addressed.

playerResponses.text: Cursed temples and angry spirits. Not my usual work, but I'll go.
Sounds like superstition. But if the land really is dying, I'll investigate.
I don't meddle with forces I don't understand. Find a priest.

bindings.quest_action: purify the desecrated shrine upstream by applying sacred materials
bindings.quest_focus: broken seals at an ancient shrine that contained the corruption
bindings.quest_stakes: anything living that touches the blackened river withers within hours
bindings.quest_threat: whatever was sealed in the shrine is awake and poisoning the valley
bindings.quest_time_pressure: the corruption spreads further each day

dialogueChunks.intro: Don't go near the water, {player_name}. Please. Not until you hear what I have to say.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Three days ago the river turned black. {quest_stakes}. The alchemists believe {quest_focus} was broken: someone let the corruption out. The only way to reverse it is with {quest_item}, applied directly to the shrine stones. I need you to {quest_action} {location_hint}. The path upstream is thick with {enemy_type} drawn to the dark energy.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_threat}. If we don't act, this entire valley will be poisoned.

playerResponses.text: Show me the old texts. I'll find the shrine and cleanse it.
Desecrated shrines and cursed rivers. What have you people been doing out here?
Ancient corruption is beyond my abilities. You need a scholar, not a sword.

bindings.quest_action: place a purification ward at the heart-tree of the old forest
bindings.quest_focus: the spirit guardians of the forest who have turned from protectors to destroyers
bindings.quest_stakes: the forest that once sheltered travelers now kills anyone who enters
bindings.quest_threat: something has corrupted sacred protectors into instruments of destruction
bindings.quest_origin: generations of guardianship twisted by an unknown corruption

dialogueChunks.intro: I've traveled far to reach {location}, {player_name}, because I heard there's someone here brave enough or foolish enough to help me.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} have turned hostile. For generations they protected travelers and kept the {enemy_type} at bay. Now {quest_stakes}. {target_npc} ventured in to leave offerings and barely escaped. {quest_threat}, and I believe {quest_item} can anchor a ward, but someone must {quest_action} {location_hint}.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're not fighting a mortal enemy. We're trying to heal something sacred. {quest_origin} demands a different kind of courage.

playerResponses.text: Healing what's sacred sounds like a cause worth risking for.
Corrupted spirits, cursed forests, certain danger. Why not.
I respect the old spirits enough to stay out of their business.
